definition: Grid modernization investment does not equal investable opportunity. 60-70% earns regulated returns (9-11% ROE), ~30% is government-funded with limited private returns, and genuinely competitive segments are <5% of headline figures (~$35-50B/yr total in storage, smart grid software, and EV charging).
evidence: Regulated utility capex earns regulated 9-11% ROE
Government-funded transmission has limited private returns
Competitive segments: grid-scale storage ~$15-20B/yr, smart grid software ~$10-15B/yr, EV charging ~$10-15B/yr
open_questions: Can electrical equipment manufacturers (Eaton, Hubbell, Schneider) sustain current order book visibility?
Will competitive segments grow as a share of total grid investment?
Does regulated utility capex offer sufficient risk-adjusted returns in a higher-rate environment?
